types.name: BorrowOrderConfigArgs
docs: A subset of [BorrowOrderConfig] excluding the accounts passed via [SetBorrowOrder].

types.name: LastUpdate
docs: Last update state

fields.name: slot
docs: Last slot when updated

fields.name: stale
docs: True when marked stale, false when slot updated

fields.name: price_status
docs: Status of the prices used to calculate the last update

fields.name: debt_reserve
docs: Mandatory debt reserve for this elevation group

types.name: BorrowOrder
docs: A borrow order.
When the [Obligation::borrow_order] is populated (i.e. non-zeroed) on an Obligation, then the
permissionless "fill" operations may borrow liquidity to the owner according to this
specification.

fields.name: debt_liquidity_mint
docs: The asset to be borrowed.
The reserves used for [Obligation::borrows] *must* all provide exactly this asset.

fields.name: remaining_debt_amount
docs: The amount of debt that still needs to be filled, in lamports.

fields.name: filled_debt_destination
docs: The token account owned by the [Obligation::owner] and holding [Self::debt_liquidity_mint],
where the filled funds should be transferred to.

fields.name: min_debt_term_seconds
docs: The minimum allowed debt term that the obligation owner agrees to.
The reserves used to fill this order *cannot* define their debt term *lower* than this.
If zeroed, then only indefinite-term reserves may be used.

fields.name: fillable_until_timestamp
docs: The time until which the borrow order can still be filled.

fields.name: placed_at_timestamp
docs: The time at which this order was placed.
Currently, this is only a piece of metadata.

fields.name: last_updated_at_timestamp
docs: The time at which this order was most-recently updated (including: created).
Currently, this is only a piece of metadata.

fields.name: requested_debt_amount
docs: The amount of debt that was originally requested when this order was most-recently updated.
In other words: this field holds a value of [Self::remaining_debt_amount] captured at
[Self::last_updated_at_timestamp].
Currently, this is only a piece of metadata.

fields.name: max_borrow_rate_bps
docs: The maximum borrow rate that the obligation owner agrees to.
The reserves used for [Obligation::borrows] *cannot* define their maximum borrow rate
*higher* than this.

fields.name: active
docs: Whether the [Self::remaining_debt_amount] is non-zero.
This field is *not* used by smart contract logic (which prefers to treat the above
[Self::remaining_debt_amount]-based definition as the single source of truth). However, it
is useful for off-chain bots (order-searchers) to efficiently list (i.e. `memcmp` filter)
just the obligations that have active borrow orders.

fields.name: padding1
docs: Alignment padding.

fields.name: end_padding
docs: End padding.

types.name: ObligationCollateral
docs: Obligation collateral state

fields.name: deposit_reserve
docs: Reserve collateral is deposited to

fields.name: deposited_amount
docs: Amount of collateral deposited

fields.name: market_value_sf
docs: Collateral market value in quote currency (scaled fraction)

fields.name: borrowed_amount_against_this_collateral_in_elevation_group
docs: Debt amount (lamport) taken against this collateral.
(only meaningful if this obligation is part of an elevation group, otherwise 0)
This is only indicative of the debt computed on the last refresh obligation.
If the obligation have multiple collateral this value is the same for all of them.

types.name: ObligationLiquidity
docs: Obligation liquidity state

fields.name: borrow_reserve
docs: Reserve liquidity is borrowed from

fields.name: cumulative_borrow_rate_bsf
docs: Borrow rate used for calculating interest (big scaled fraction)

fields.name: first_borrowed_at_timestamp
docs: The timestamp at which this debt was taken.
More specifically: when the *first* borrow operation from this reserve happened.
This means that:
- adding debt of the same reserve does *not* change this timestamp,
- repaying the entire debt of this reserve *does* reset this timestamp.
Note: this field is *not* only metadata: it is used in the logic, e.g. for enforcing the
fixed-term borrows (i.e. those induced by [ReserveConfig::debt_term_seconds]).

fields.name: borrowed_amount_sf
docs: Amount of liquidity borrowed plus interest (scaled fraction)

fields.name: market_value_sf
docs: Liquidity market value in quote currency (scaled fraction)

fields.name: borrow_factor_adjusted_market_value_sf
docs: Risk adjusted liquidity market value in quote currency - DEBUG ONLY - use market_value instead

fields.name: borrowed_amount_outside_elevation_groups
docs: Amount of liquidity borrowed outside of an elevation group

types.name: ObligationOrder
docs: A single obligation order.
See [Obligation::obligation_orders].

fields.name: condition_threshold_sf
docs: A threshold value used by the condition (scaled [Fraction]).
The exact meaning depends on the specific [Self::condition_type].
Examples:
- when `condition_type == 2 (UserLtvBelow)`:
then a value of `0.455` here means that the order is active only when the obligation's
user LTV is less than `0.455` (i.e. < 45.5%).
- when `condition_type == 3 (DebtCollPriceRatioAbove)`:
assuming the obligation uses BTC collateral for SOL debt, then a value of `491.3` here
means that the order is active only when the BTC-SOL price is greater than `491.3` (i.e.
> 491.3 SOL per BTC).

fields.name: opportunity_parameter_sf
docs: A configuration parameter used by the opportunity (scaled [Fraction]).
The exact meaning depends on the specific [Self::opportunity_type].
Examples:
- when `opportunity_type == 0 (DeleverageSingleDebtAmount)`:
Assuming the obligation uses BTC collateral for SOL debt, then a value of `1_234_000_000`
here means that a liquidator may repay up to 1234000000 lamports (i.e. 1.234 SOL) on this
obligation.
Note: the special value of [Fraction::MAX] is *not* allowed in this case.
- when `opportunity_type == 1 (DeleverageAllDebtAmount)`:
The only allowed value in this case is [Fraction::MAX] (to emphasize that *all* debt
should be repaid).

fields.name: min_execution_bonus_bps
docs: A *minimum* additional fraction of collateral transferred to the liquidator, in bps.
The minimum bonus is applied exactly when the [Self::condition_threshold_sf] is met, and
grows linearly towards the [Self::max_execution_bonus_bps].
Example: a value of `50` here means 50bps == 0.5% bonus for an "LTV > 65%" order, when
executed precisely at the moment LTV exceeds 65%.

fields.name: max_execution_bonus_bps
docs: A *maximum* additional fraction of collateral transferred to the liquidator, in bps.
The maximum bonus is applied at the relevant "extreme" state of the obligation, i.e.:
- for a stop-loss condition, it is a point at which the obligation becomes liquidatable;
- for a take-profit condition, it is a point at which obligation has 0% LTV.
In non-extreme states, the actual bonus value is interpolated linearly, starting from
[Self::min_execution_bonus_bps] (at the point specified by the order's condition).
Example: a value of `300` here means 300bps == 3.0% bonus for a "debt/coll price > 140"
order, when executed at a higher price = 200, at which the obligation's LTV happens to
be equal to its liquidation LTV.

fields.name: condition_type
docs: Serialized [ConditionType].
The entire order is void when this is zeroed (i.e. representing [ConditionType::Never]).
Example: a value of `2` here denotes `UserLtvBelow` condition type. Of course, to
interpret this condition, we also need to take the [Self::condition_threshold_sf] into
account.

fields.name: opportunity_type
docs: Serialized [OpportunityType].
Example: a value of `0` here denotes `DeleverageSingleDebtAmount` opportunity. Of course, to
interpret this opportunity, we also need to take the [Self::opportunity_parameter_sf] into
account.

fields.name: padding1
docs: Alignment padding.
The fields above take up 2+2+1+1 bytes = 48 bits, which means we need 80 bits = 10 bytes to
align with `u128`s.

fields.name: padding2
docs: End padding.
The total size of a single instance is 8*u128 = 128 bytes.

types.name: FeeCalculation
docs: Calculate fees exlusive or inclusive of an amount

types.name: ReserveCollateral
docs: Reserve collateral

fields.name: mint_pubkey
docs: Reserve collateral mint address

fields.name: mint_total_supply
docs: Reserve collateral mint supply, used for exchange rate

fields.name: supply_vault
docs: Reserve collateral supply address

types.name: ReserveConfig
docs: Reserve configuration values

fields.name: status
docs: Status of the reserve Active/Obsolete/Hidden

fields.name: padding_deprecated_asset_tier
docs: Asset tier -> 0 - regular (collateral & debt), 1 - isolated collateral, 2 - isolated debt

fields.name: host_fixed_interest_rate_bps
docs: Flat rate that goes to the host

fields.name: min_deleveraging_bonus_bps
docs: Starting bonus for deleveraging-related liquidations, in bps.

fields.name: block_ctoken_usage
docs: Boolean flag to block minting/redeeming of ctokens
Blocks usage of ctokens (minting or withdrawing from obligation)
Effectively blocks deposit_reserve_liquidity and withdraw_obligation_collateral

fields.name: reserved1
docs: Past reserved space - feel free to reuse.

fields.name: protocol_order_execution_fee_pct
docs: Cut of the order execution bonus that the protocol receives, as a percentage

fields.name: protocol_take_rate_pct
docs: Protocol take rate is the amount borrowed interest protocol receives, as a percentage

fields.name: protocol_liquidation_fee_pct
docs: Cut of the liquidation bonus that the protocol receives, as a percentage

fields.name: loan_to_value_pct
docs: Target ratio of the value of borrows to deposits, as a percentage
0 if use as collateral is disabled

fields.name: liquidation_threshold_pct
docs: Loan to value ratio at which an obligation can be liquidated, as percentage

fields.name: min_liquidation_bonus_bps
docs: Minimum bonus a liquidator receives when repaying part of an unhealthy obligation, as bps

fields.name: max_liquidation_bonus_bps
docs: Maximum bonus a liquidator receives when repaying part of an unhealthy obligation, as bps

fields.name: bad_debt_liquidation_bonus_bps
docs: Bad debt liquidation bonus for an undercollateralized obligation, as bps

fields.name: deleveraging_margin_call_period_secs
docs: Time in seconds that must pass before redemptions are enabled after the deposit limit is
crossed.
Only relevant when `autodeleverage_enabled == 1`, and must not be 0 in such case.

fields.name: deleveraging_threshold_decrease_bps_per_day
docs: The rate at which the deleveraging threshold decreases, in bps per day.
Only relevant when `autodeleverage_enabled == 1`, and must not be 0 in such case.

fields.name: fees
docs: Program owner fees assessed, separate from gains due to interest accrual

fields.name: borrow_rate_curve
docs: Borrow rate curve based on utilization

fields.name: borrow_factor_pct
docs: Borrow factor in percentage - used for risk adjustment

fields.name: deposit_limit
docs: Maximum deposit limit of liquidity in native units, u64::MAX for inf

fields.name: borrow_limit
docs: Maximum amount borrowed, u64::MAX for inf, 0 to disable borrows (protected deposits)

fields.name: token_info
docs: Token id from TokenInfos struct

fields.name: deposit_withdrawal_cap
docs: Deposit withdrawal caps - deposit & redeem

fields.name: debt_withdrawal_cap
docs: Debt withdrawal caps - borrow & repay

fields.name: utilization_limit_block_borrowing_above_pct
docs: Utilization (in percentage) above which borrowing is blocked. 0 to disable.

fields.name: autodeleverage_enabled
docs: Whether this reserve should be subject to auto-deleveraging after deposit or borrow limit is
crossed.
Besides this flag, the lending market's flag also needs to be enabled (logical `AND`).
**NOTE:** the manual "target LTV" deleveraging is NOT affected by this flag.

fields.name: proposer_authority_locked
docs: Boolean flag indicating whether the reserve is locked for the proposer authority.
Once the proposer have finished preparing the reserve, it must be locked to prevent
further changes to the reserve configuration allowing review and voting on the proposal
without alteration during the voting period.

fields.name: borrow_limit_outside_elevation_group
docs: Maximum amount liquidity of this reserve borrowed outside all elevation groups
- u64::MAX for inf
- 0 to disable borrows outside elevation groups

fields.name: borrow_limit_against_this_collateral_in_elevation_group
docs: Defines the maximum amount (in lamports of elevation group debt asset)
that can be borrowed when this reserve is used as collateral.
- u64::MAX for inf
- 0 to disable borrows in this elevation group (expected value for the debt asset)

fields.name: deleveraging_bonus_increase_bps_per_day
docs: The rate at which the deleveraging-related liquidation bonus increases, in bps per day.
Only relevant when `autodeleverage_enabled == 1`, and must not be 0 in such case.

fields.name: debt_maturity_timestamp
docs: The timestamp at which all [Obligation::borrows] using this reserve become liquidatable
(on the same terms as reserve-wide deleveraging).
Inactive when zeroed (i.e. debt never matures).
Note: this feature is independent of [Self::debt_term_seconds] - the liquidation mechanism
is based directly on the timestamp defined here, on Reserve's level.

fields.name: debt_term_seconds
docs: The duration after which any debt coming from this Reserve must be repaid.
Inactive when zeroed (i.e. funds can be borrowed indefinitely).
Note: this feature is independent of [Self::debt_maturity_timestamp] - the liquidation
mechanism is based on the [ObligationLiquidity::first_borrowed_at_timestamp].

types.name: ReserveFees
docs: Additional fee information on a reserve
These exist separately from interest accrual fees, and are specifically for the program owner
and referral fee. The fees are paid out as a percentage of liquidity token amounts during
repayments and liquidations.

fields.name: origination_fee_sf
docs: Fee assessed on `BorrowObligationLiquidity`, as scaled fraction (60 bits fractional part)
Must be between `0` and `2^60`, such that `2^60 = 1`.  A few examples for
clarity:
0.00001% (Aave origination fee) = 115292150461

fields.name: flash_loan_fee_sf
docs: Fee for flash loan, expressed as scaled fraction.

fields.name: padding
docs: Used for allignment

types.name: ReserveLiquidity
docs: Reserve liquidity

fields.name: mint_pubkey
docs: Reserve liquidity mint address

fields.name: supply_vault
docs: Reserve liquidity supply address

fields.name: fee_vault
docs: Reserve liquidity fee collection address

fields.name: total_available_amount
docs: Total reserve liquidity available.
Note: not all of this liquidity can be freely used for any purpose. Production code should
use the specialized getters - see e.g. [Reserve::total_available_liquidity_amount()],
[Reserve::freely_available_liquidity_amount()].

fields.name: borrowed_amount_sf
docs: Reserve liquidity borrowed (scaled fraction)

fields.name: market_price_sf
docs: Reserve liquidity market price in quote currency (scaled fraction)

fields.name: market_price_last_updated_ts
docs: Unix timestamp of the market price (from the oracle)

fields.name: mint_decimals
docs: Reserve liquidity mint decimals

fields.name: deposit_limit_crossed_timestamp
docs: Timestamp when the last refresh reserve detected that the liquidity amount is above the deposit cap. When this threshold is crossed, then redemptions (auto-deleverage) are enabled.
If the threshold is not crossed, then the timestamp is set to 0

fields.name: borrow_limit_crossed_timestamp
docs: Timestamp when the last refresh reserve detected that the borrowed amount is above the borrow cap. When this threshold is crossed, then redemptions (auto-deleverage) are enabled.
If the threshold is not crossed, then the timestamp is set to 0

fields.name: cumulative_borrow_rate_bsf
docs: Reserve liquidity cumulative borrow rate (scaled fraction)

fields.name: accumulated_protocol_fees_sf
docs: Reserve cumulative protocol fees (scaled fraction)

fields.name: accumulated_referrer_fees_sf
docs: Reserve cumulative referrer fees (scaled fraction)

fields.name: pending_referrer_fees_sf
docs: Reserve pending referrer fees, to be claimed in refresh_obligation by referrer or protocol (scaled fraction)

fields.name: absolute_referral_rate_sf
docs: Reserve referrer fee absolute rate calculated at each refresh_reserve operation (scaled fraction)

fields.name: token_program
docs: Token program of the liquidity mint

types.name: WithdrawQueue
docs: A tracker of ticket-based withdrawals.

fields.name: queued_collateral_amount
docs: The part of [ReserveLiquidity::total_available_amount] locked for ticketed withdrawals.

fields.name: next_issued_ticket_sequence_number
docs: The sequence number of the next ticket to be issued when enqueueing to withdraw.
Note: it is also a number of tickets issued so far.

fields.name: next_withdrawable_ticket_sequence_number
docs: The sequence number of the next ticket to be used for actually transferring the withdrawn
liquidity (assuming it is available in the reserve).
Note: it is also a number of fully-consumed tickets so far.

types.name: WithdrawalCaps
docs: Reserve Withdrawal Caps State

fields.name: lower
docs: Lower value of acceptable price

fields.name: upper
docs: Upper value of acceptable price

fields.name: exp
docs: Number of decimals of the previously defined values

fields.name: price
docs: Pubkey of the base price feed (disabled if `null` or `default`)

fields.name: price_feed
docs: Pubkey of the scope price feed (disabled if `null` or `default`)

fields.name: price_chain
docs: This is the scope_id price chain that results in a price for the token

fields.name: twap_chain
docs: This is the scope_id price chain for the twap

fields.name: price_aggregator
docs: Pubkey of the base price feed (disabled if `null` or `default`)

fields.name: name
docs: UTF-8 encoded name of the token (null-terminated)

fields.name: heuristic
docs: Heuristics limits of acceptable price

fields.name: max_twap_divergence_bps
docs: Max divergence between twap and price in bps

fields.name: scope_configuration
docs: Scope price configuration

fields.name: switchboard_configuration
docs: Switchboard configuration

fields.name: pyth_configuration
docs: Pyth configuration

fields.name: global_admin
docs: Global admin of the program

fields.name: pending_admin
docs: Pending admin must sign a specific transaction to become the global admin

fields.name: fee_collector
docs: Fee collector is the only allowed owner of token accounts receiving protocol fees

fields.name: padding
docs: Padding to make the struct size 1024 bytes

fields.name: version
docs: Version of lending market

fields.name: bump_seed
docs: Bump seed for derived authority address

fields.name: lending_market_owner
docs: Owner authority which can add new reserves

fields.name: lending_market_owner_cached
docs: Temporary cache of the lending market owner, used in update_lending_market_owner

fields.name: quote_currency
docs: Currency market prices are quoted in

fields.name: referral_fee_bps
docs: Referral fee for the lending market, as bps out of the total protocol fee

fields.name: autodeleverage_enabled
docs: Whether the obligations on this market should be subject to auto-deleveraging after deposit
or borrow limit is crossed.
Besides this flag, the particular reserve's flag also needs to be enabled (logical `AND`).
**NOTE:** this also affects the individual "target LTV" deleveraging.

fields.name: price_refresh_trigger_to_max_age_pct
docs: Refresh price from oracle only if it's older than this percentage of the price max age.
e.g. if the max age is set to 100s and this is set to 80%, the price will be refreshed if it's older than 80s.
Price is always refreshed if this set to 0.

fields.name: liquidation_max_debt_close_factor_pct
docs: Percentage of the total borrowed value in an obligation available for liquidation

fields.name: insolvency_risk_unhealthy_ltv_pct
docs: Minimum acceptable unhealthy LTV before max_debt_close_factor_pct becomes 100%

fields.name: min_full_liquidation_value_threshold
docs: Minimum liquidation value threshold triggering full liquidation for an obligation, in full
units of the quote currency (e.g. `2` means "$2", not "2 lamports of USDC").

fields.name: max_liquidatable_debt_market_value_at_once
docs: Max allowed liquidation value in one ix call

fields.name: reserved0
docs: [DEPRECATED] Global maximum unhealthy borrow value allowed for any obligation

fields.name: global_allowed_borrow_value
docs: Global maximum allowed borrow value allowed for any obligation

fields.name: emergency_council
docs: The address of the emergency council, in charge of taking emergency actions on the market (e.g., enabling emergency mode)

fields.name: reserved1
docs: [DEPRECATED] Reward points multiplier per obligation type

fields.name: elevation_groups
docs: Elevation groups are used to group together reserves that have the same risk parameters and can bump the ltv and liquidation threshold

fields.name: min_net_value_in_obligation_sf
docs: Min net value accepted to be found in a position after any lending action in an obligation (scaled by quote currency decimals)

fields.name: min_value_skip_liquidation_ltv_checks
docs: Minimum value to enforce smallest ltv priority checks on the collateral reserves on liquidation

fields.name: name
docs: Market name, zero-padded.

fields.name: min_value_skip_liquidation_bf_checks
docs: Minimum value to enforce highest borrow factor priority checks on the debt reserves on liquidation

fields.name: individual_autodeleverage_margin_call_period_secs
docs: Time (in seconds) that must pass before liquidation is allowed on an obligation that has
been individually marked for auto-deleveraging.

fields.name: min_initial_deposit_amount
docs: Minimum amount of deposit at creation of a reserve to prevent artificial inflation
Note: this amount cannot be recovered, the ctoken associated are never minted

fields.name: obligation_order_execution_enabled
docs: Whether the obligation orders should be evaluated during liquidations.

fields.name: immutable
docs: Whether the lending market is set as immutable.

fields.name: obligation_order_creation_enabled
docs: Whether new obligation orders can be created.
Note: updating or cancelling existing orders is *not* affected by this flag.

fields.name: price_triggered_liquidation_disabled
docs: Whether the liquidation operations that are triggered by price changes should be disabled.
This includes regular liquidation (i.e. LTV exceeding the unhealthy threshold) and some
obligation orders' execution.
*Caution:* this flag is *disabling* the liquidations when `1` - contrary to all the other
liquidation-driving flags (see e.g. [Self::autodeleverage_enabled]).

fields.name: mature_reserve_debt_liquidation_enabled
docs: Whether the debts that reached their reserve's [ReserveConfig::debt_maturity_timestamp] can
be liquidated.

fields.name: obligation_borrow_debt_term_liquidation_enabled
docs: Whether the [Obligation::borrows] that reached their [ReserveConfig::debt_term_seconds] can
be liquidated.

fields.name: borrow_order_creation_enabled
docs: Whether new borrow orders can be created.
Note: updating or cancelling existing orders is *not* affected by this flag.

fields.name: borrow_order_execution_enabled
docs: Whether the existing borrow orders can be filled.

fields.name: proposer_authority
docs: Authority that can propose creating of new reserves but cannot enable them.

fields.name: min_borrow_order_fill_value
docs: Minimum value that can be filled in a single `fill_borrow_order()` call, in full units of
the quote currency (e.g. `2` means "$2", not "2 lamports of USDC").

fields.name: withdraw_ticket_issuance_enabled
docs: Whether any new withdraw tickets can be issued (i.e. whether new requests can enter the
withdraw queue).

fields.name: withdraw_ticket_redemption_enabled
docs: Whether the existing withdraw tickets can be redeemed (i.e. whether the tickets can be used
to transfer accumulated pending liquidity to destination accounts).

fields.name: min_withdraw_queued_liquidity_value
docs: Minimum value that can be withdrawn in a single `withdraw_queued_liquidity()` call, in full
units of the quote currency (e.g. `2` means "$2", not "2 lamports of USDC").

types.name: Obligation
docs: Lending market obligation state

fields.name: tag
docs: Version of the struct

fields.name: last_update
docs: Last update to collateral, liquidity, or their market values

fields.name: lending_market
docs: Lending market address

fields.name: owner
docs: Owner authority which can borrow liquidity

fields.name: deposits
docs: Deposited collateral for the obligation, unique by deposit reserve address

fields.name: lowest_reserve_deposit_liquidation_ltv
docs: Worst LTV for the collaterals backing the loan, represented as a percentage

fields.name: deposited_value_sf
docs: Market value of deposits (scaled fraction)

fields.name: borrows
docs: Borrowed liquidity for the obligation, unique by borrow reserve address

fields.name: borrow_factor_adjusted_debt_value_sf
docs: Risk adjusted market value of borrows/debt (sum of price * borrowed_amount * borrow_factor) (scaled fraction)

fields.name: borrowed_assets_market_value_sf
docs: Market value of borrows - used for max_liquidatable_borrowed_amount (scaled fraction)

fields.name: allowed_borrow_value_sf
docs: The maximum borrow value at the weighted average loan to value ratio (scaled fraction)

fields.name: unhealthy_borrow_value_sf
docs: The dangerous borrow value at the weighted average liquidation threshold (scaled fraction)

fields.name: padding_deprecated_asset_tiers
docs: The asset tier of the deposits

fields.name: elevation_group
docs: The elevation group id the obligation opted into.

fields.name: num_of_obsolete_deposit_reserves
docs: The number of obsolete reserves the obligation has a deposit in

fields.name: has_debt
docs: Marked = 1 if borrows array is not empty, 0 = borrows empty

fields.name: referrer
docs: Wallet address of the referrer

fields.name: borrowing_disabled
docs: Marked = 1 if borrowing disabled, 0 = borrowing enabled

fields.name: autodeleverage_target_ltv_pct
docs: A target LTV set by the market owner when marking this obligation for deleveraging.
Only effective when `deleveraging_margin_call_started_slot != 0`.

fields.name: lowest_reserve_deposit_max_ltv_pct
docs: The lowest max LTV found amongst the collateral deposits

fields.name: num_of_obsolete_borrow_reserves
docs: The number of obsolete reserves the obligation has a borrow in

fields.name: autodeleverage_margin_call_started_timestamp
docs: A timestamp at which the market owner most-recently marked this obligation for deleveraging.
Zero if not currently subject to deleveraging.

fields.name: obligation_orders
docs: Owner-defined, permissionlessly-executed repay orders.
Typical use-cases would be a stop-loss and a take-profit (possibly co-existing).

fields.name: borrow_order
docs: Owner-defined, permissionlessly-executed borrow order applicable to this obligation.
Non-zeroed only on a newly-initialized fixed-rate, fixed-term obligation.

types.name: ReferrerTokenState
docs: Referrer account -> each owner can have multiple accounts for specific reserves

fields.name: referrer
docs: Pubkey of the referrer/owner

fields.name: mint
docs: Token mint for the account

fields.name: amount_unclaimed_sf
docs: Amount that has been accumulated and not claimed yet -> available to claim (scaled fraction)

fields.name: amount_cumulative_sf
docs: Amount that has been accumulated in total -> both already claimed and unclaimed (scaled fraction)

fields.name: bump
docs: Referrer token state bump, used for address validation

types.name: UserMetadata
docs: Referrer account -> each owner can have multiple accounts for specific reserves

fields.name: referrer
docs: Pubkey of the referrer/owner - pubkey::default if no referrer

fields.name: bump
docs: Bump used for validation of account address

fields.name: user_lookup_table
docs: User lookup table - used to store all user accounts - atas for each reserve mint, each obligation PDA, UserMetadata itself and all referrer_token_states if there is a referrer

fields.name: owner
docs: User metadata account owner

fields.name: version
docs: Version of the reserve

fields.name: last_update
docs: Last slot when supply and rates updated

fields.name: lending_market
docs: Lending market address

fields.name: liquidity
docs: Reserve liquidity

fields.name: collateral
docs: Reserve collateral

fields.name: config
docs: Reserve configuration values

fields.name: borrowed_amounts_against_this_reserve_in_elevation_groups
docs: Amount of token borrowed in lamport of debt asset in the given
elevation group when this reserve is part of the collaterals.

fields.name: withdraw_queue
docs: The tracker of ticket-based withdrawals.

types.name: WithdrawTicket
docs: A finite-lifecycle account representing a specific depositor's place in the withdraw queue of
a specific reserve.
The lifecycle:
1. The depositor holding ctokens wants to withdraw funds from the reserve, and finds out that
the required amount is not available (due to high utilization).
2. The depositor calls the `enqueue_to_withdraw` handler.
3. The handler transfers the depositor's ctokens to the reserve's internal "pending" vault.
4. The handler initializes a new [WithdrawTicket] account, with the next available sequence
number.
5. The depositor waits until his ticket is the next expected one for actual withdraw, and until
the reserve has enough liquidity.
6. Anyone (the depositor or a bot) calls the permissionless `withdraw_queued_liquidity`
handler. If the ticket became invalid (e.g. destination account no longer exists), then the
depositor can call the `recover_invalid_ticket_collateral` handler instead.
7. The handler transfers the liquidity amount according to the current exchange rate.
8. The handler closes the ticket account.

fields.name: sequence_number
docs: This ticket's place in the queue; the same as used for PDA derivation.

fields.name: owner
docs: The funds' owner (the user who called the `enqueue_to_withdraw` handler).

fields.name: reserve
docs: The reserve to withdraw from.

fields.name: user_destination_liquidity_ta
docs: The token account to which the finally-available liquidity should be transferred (by the
`withdraw_queued_liquidity` handler).

fields.name: queued_collateral_amount
docs: The amount of collateral still waiting to be withdrawn using this ticket.

fields.name: created_at_timestamp
docs: The timestamp at which the queue was entered.
This is currently only a piece of metadata, not used by the logic.

fields.name: invalid
docs: Whether the ticket has been found to be invalid (e.g. the [Self::user_destination_liquidity]
has been repurposed) by the `withdraw_queued_liquidity` handler.
To be specific: valid = `0`, invalid = `1`.
An invalid ticket cannot be made valid again, and can only be passed to the
`recover_invalid_ticket_collateral` handler.

fields.name: alignment_padding
docs: Inner padding, for alignment.

fields.name: end_padding
docs: Trailing padding, for future developments.
